title: carex pseudocyperus subsp. haenkeana
intro: Carex pseudocyperus subsp. haenkeana, also known as Haenke's sedge, is a plant species within the Carex genus. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can be attributed to its chemical composition. The health benefits of Haenke's sedge include its potential to treat various infections, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in the form of infusions or decoctions, often used to treat coughs and bronchitis, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
